steps.title: History & Chief Complaint
steps.description: Gather patient timeline, localized knee pain symptoms, and impact on daily functioning.
expected_findings: Gradual or insidious onset of deep, dull aching pain in the knee joint during weight bearing
Morning stiffness lasting less than 30 minutes, resolving with activity
Pain temporarily relieved by rest and aggravated by prolonged walking or stair climbing
Difficulty with functional tasks like squatting, kneeling, rising from a low chair

steps.title: Inspection in Standing and Walking
steps.description: Assess visual lower limb alignment, deformities, muscular asymmetry, and gait patterns.
expected_findings: Genu varum (bow-legs) alignment from asymmetric medial cartilage loss
Atrophy/wasting of the Quadriceps muscle, specifically the Vastus Medialis Obliquus (VMO)
Mild-to-moderate localized joint swelling, bony enlargement, or joint capsule thickening
Antalgic gait with shortened stance phase on the affected limb to avoid pain

steps.title: Palpation of Joint Structures
steps.description: Identify localized heat, localized joint effusion, osteophytes, and painful landmarks.
expected_findings: Exquisite tenderness along the medial or lateral knee joint line
Palpable bony hypertrophic ridges (osteophytes) along tibial or femoral margins
Normal or slightly elevated local skin temperature (indicating mild active synovitis)
Coarse patellofemoral crepitus during active or passive knee flexion and extension

steps.title: Range of Motion Assessment
steps.description: Check active and passive motion curves of the knee to locate restriction limits.
expected_findings: Painful active and passive knee flexion restricted to less than 110 degrees
Inability to achieve complete knee extension (extension lag of 5-10 degrees)
Passive knee flexion exhibiting a hard capsular or bony end-feel
Asymmetrical side-to-side end-range pain patterns during extension

steps.title: Manual Muscle Testing
steps.description: Grade strength of major stabilizers and prime movers about the knee and hip.
expected_findings: Quadriceps strength reduced to Grade 3 or 4 (arthrogenous muscle inhibition secondary to pain)
Hamstrings strength demonstrating Grade 4 (disuse weakness and defense guarding)
Weak hip abductors (Gluteus Medius Grade 4) causing pelvic drop on contralateral side during single-limb support

steps.title: Provocative Special Tests
steps.description: Inject mechanical stresses to verify effusion, meniscus or patellofemoral cartilage disruption.
expected_findings: Positive Patellar Tap test indicating moderate-to-large knee joint effusion
Positive Fluctuation test confirming minor extra-synovial fluid
Positive Clarke's Test (Patellar Grind) producing retropatellar clicking or pain
Negative knee ligament laxity tests (Lachman, Anterior and Posterior Drawer) ruling out acute ACL/PCL tears

steps.title: Functional Capacity Outcomes
steps.description: Synthesize scores of formal functional scales and active transfer transitions.
expected_findings: Elevated WOMAC index scores indicating highly compromised pain, stiffness, and daily activities
Pain intensity scaled between 5/10 and 8/10 on the Numeric Pain Rating Scale (NPRS)
Compensatory upper limb push-off required during sit-to-stand transfers

steps.title: Differential Reasoning & Goal Settings
steps.description: Establish clinical correlations from primary assessment findings to draft target parameters.
expected_findings: Impairment list completed: medial compartment OA, Quadriceps/VMO weakness, knee flexion contractures
Short-term goals: reduce weight-bearing pain to NPRS < 4/10, increase VMO activation, restore terminal extension in 2 weeks
Long-term goals: complete independent community ambulation with minimal load pain, restore complete Quadriceps strength to Grade 5 in 6 weeks

steps.title: Chronology of Shoulder Symptoms
steps.description: Trace symptom progression across the freezing, frozen, and thawing stages.
expected_findings: Gradual, insidious onset of unilateral shoulder ache with localizing radiation into the deltoid region
Inability to lie on the affected side at night with significant sleep interruption
No history of concrete major trauma, though minor microtrauma or microvascular disease (diabetes) may be noted
Progressive global loss of movement preventing basic hygiene, grooming, and clothing tasks

steps.title: Postural Inspection of Girdle
steps.description: Inspect dynamic alignment, joint default postures, and atrophy.
expected_findings: Afflicted shoulder held in a protective default posture of adduction, internal rotation, and elevation
Asymmetrical shoulder hiking during early active elevation
Wasting or flattening of the Deltoid, Supraspinatus, and Infraspinatus muscle borders
Slight compensatory thoracic spine kyphosis to unload shoulder extension

steps.title: Palpation of Girdle Tissues
steps.description: Identify local tissue guarding, high muscle tone, and focal tenderness points.
expected_findings: Focal tenderness over the anterior joint capsule, subacromial space, and bicipital groove
Severe protective spasm/hypertonicity of the Upper Trapezius, Levator Scapulae, and Pectoralis Major
Firm, inelastic feeling (capsular tightening) over the anterior joint margin
No elevated localized heat or erythema (rules out active septic process)

steps.title: Range of Motion Metrics
steps.description: Establish degree of active and passive restrictions across all axes of glenohumeral movement.
expected_findings: Classical capsular pattern of restriction: External Rotation is most limited, followed by Abduction, Flexion, and then Internal Rotation
Firm, abrupt, leathery capsular end-feel during passive range testing
Severe pain elicited at the end-range limits of all passive motions
Significant restriction of passive glenohumeral motion with excessive accessory scapulothoracic compensation

steps.title: Manual Muscle Testing of Shoulder Girdle
steps.description: Grade isolated force curves of the rotator cuff and scapular organizers.
expected_findings: Supraspinatus, Infraspinatus, and Subscapularis testing as Grade 4 (inhibited by joint pain, rather than true nerve injury)
Scapular stabilizers (Middle/Lower Trapezius, Serratus Anterior) showing Grade 4 strength
Upper Trapezius proving overactive and hypertonic on manual muscle resistance

steps.title: Provocative Impingement & Cuff Integrity Tests
steps.description: Apply maneuvers to analyze subacromial space patency and ruling out primary structural cuff tears.
expected_findings: Markedly altered Scapulohumeral Rhythm with a 1:1 motion ratio (scapular rotation occurs almost immediately)
Painful, limited Neer and Hawkins-Kennedy tests due to global capsular confinement
Negative Drop Arm test and Empty Can test for structural tears (though painful, arm maintains hold)

steps.title: Functional Performance scales
steps.description: Measure shoulder function, self-care restrictions, and score diagnostic questionnaires.
expected_findings: DASH (Disabilities of the Arm, Shoulder, and Hand) score exceeding 55%, indicating severe ADL disability
Positive bilaterally restricted Apley's Scratch tests (inability to reach base of neck and lower lumbar spine)
Pain rated 7/10 on the NPRS during overhead reaches

steps.title: Rehabilitation Phase Diagnostics
steps.description: Differentiate stages to guide joint mobilization parameters.
expected_findings: Identification of staging (e.g., Frozen stage, showing rigid limits with low irritability vs Freezing stage with hyper-irritability)
Rehab plan planned: Grade I/II oscillations for pain control in early high-irritability stages; Grade III/IV stretches for remodeling in low-irritability stages
Home exercise compliance verified: pulley exercises, finger ladder, and sleeper stretch

steps.title: History & Sciatic Pain Character
steps.description: Chronicle immediate mechanics of injury (bending, twisting, lifting) and radiate symptoms mapping.
expected_findings: Sudden onset of sharp, electric, shooting pain radiating from the low back into the gluteal area, posterior thigh, calf, and foot
Unilateral paresthesia, numbness, or pins-and-needles mirroring L5 or S1 dermatomes
Radicular pain aggravated by trunk flexion, prolonged sitting, coughing, or sneezing (valsalva pressure)
Pain temporarily relieved by walking, standing, or lying prone (extension preference)

steps.title: Visual Spinal Curvature
steps.description: Inspect posture in sagittal and coronal axes and transitions.
expected_findings: Flattening or reversal of normal lumbar lordosis from protective paraspinal spasm
Lateral shift of the trunk (ipsilateral or contralateral list) to displace neural compression
Guarded/stiff sagittal trunk adjustments when changing positions
Antalgic gait avoiding complete terminal hip/knee extension of the affected leg to suppress neural tension

steps.title: Segmental Palpation of Spine
steps.description: Palpate vertebral landmarks, myofascial triggers, and sciatic pathway points.
expected_findings: Focal tenderness over L4-L5 or L5-S1 interspinous landmarks
Spastic hypertonicity in bilateral Lumbar Erector Spinae and Quadratus Lumborum
Reproduced radiating nerve ache on palpation of Valleix sciatic trigger points
Presence of active myofascial trigger points in the Gluteus Medius, Minimus, and Piriformis

steps.title: Active Trunk Mobility Bounds
steps.description: Determine range limits of lumbar flex, extend, and side bend.
expected_findings: Trunk flexion restricted to less than 20 degrees, provoking peripheralization of leg pain
Trunk extension restricted but yielding centralization of symptoms (pain retracting to lumbar region)
Lateral bending toward the affected side restricted by sudden radicular compression

steps.title: Segmental Myotome Mapping
steps.description: Grade strength of specific nerve root-controlled muscles across the ankles and toes.
expected_findings: Weakness in the L5 myotome: Extensor Hallucis Longus (EHL) showing Grade 3 or 4 strength
Weakness in the S1 myotome: Gastrocnemius/Soleus or Peroneus Longus/Brevis showing Grade 4 strength
Weakness in the L4 myotome: Tibialis Anterior showing Grade 4

steps.title: Dural Tension special Tests
steps.description: Utilize dural elongation tests to provoke pain from disc-root conflict.
expected_findings: Positive Straight Leg Raise (SLR) test recreating electric radiating pain between 30 and 70 degrees
Positive Lasegue sign confirming dural origin (distal pain is aggravated by ankle dorsiflexion)
Positive Slump test replicating thoracic, lumbar, and calf sciatic pain under cervical and thoracic flexion
Positive Crossed SLR test (raising the normal leg provokes pain in the symptomatic leg, indicating medial disc herniation)

steps.title: Sensory, Reflex, & Disability Metrics
steps.description: Standardize sensory loss, tendon reflex depression, and grade patient disability questionnaires.
expected_findings: Diminished or absent Achilles tendon reflex (S1 nerve root) or diminished Patellar reflex (L4 root)
Sensory loss (decreased sharp touch) in L5 (dorsum of foot) or S1 (lateral border of foot) dermatomes
Oswestry Disability Index (ODI) score of 40% to 60%, showing moderate-to-severe lumbar functional disability

steps.title: Diagnostic Synthesis & Stabilizations
steps.description: Formulate core classification and design physical target benchmarks.
expected_findings: Identified level (e.g., L4-L5 herniation compressing L5 nerve root) with clear direction of bias (extension bias)
Clinical reasoning: determine whether condition is safe for stabilization training, traction, or exhibits red flags (cauda equina) requiring sudden surgery referral
Stabilization goals: posture education, transverse abdominis/multifidus dynamic motor training, neural mobilizing slides

steps.title: Vascular Onset profile
steps.description: Establish timing of ischemic/hemorrhagic stroke events, baseline functional level, and language capacity.
expected_findings: Contralateral motor weakness/paralysis, face, and arm affected more severely than the leg
Aphasia (Broca's expressive, Wernicke's receptive) or dysarthria if dominant hemisphere is involved
Sensory deficits, homonymous hemianopia, or hemispatial neglect if non-dominant hemisphere is involved
Identified clinical risk factors: hypertension, diabetes mellitus, hyperlipidemia, cardiac arrhythmias

steps.title: Spontaneous Posturing & Facies
steps.description: Inspect resting tone alignments, structural asymmetries, hemiplegic postures, and hemispatial inattention.
expected_findings: Facial asymmetry: lowering of the corner of the mouth on the contralateral side, saving the forehead wrinkles
Pronator drift or persistent flexor synergy posturing of the upper limb
Extended knee, plantarflexed/inverted ankle synergy posturing of the lower limb
Asymmetrical weight-bearing with pelvic retraction, trunk lateral flexion towards the hemiplegic side

steps.title: Muscle Tone & Reflex Testing
steps.description: Quantify muscular spasticity levels and investigate tendon reflex briskness.
expected_findings: Velocity-dependent increase in tone during passive stretch (clasp-knife/spasticity catch-and-release)
Elevated Modified Ashworth Scale (MAS) scores (e.g. Grade 1+ or 2 in flexors like biceps, calf muscles)
Brisk deep tendon reflexes (clonus-like hyperreflexia) of the contralateral limbs
Flaccidity (hypotonia) observed instead if the assessment is occurring inside the acute, transient spinal shock phase

steps.title: Passive Motion Limits & Tightness
steps.description: Measure complete passive ROM of locked joints to distinguish neural spasticity from structural contractures.
expected_findings: Limitation in glenohumeral abduction (< 90 deg) and external rotation due to subscapularis/pectoralis spastic rigidity
Limited active/passive ankle dorsiflexion, presenting with a tight, non-yielding Achilles tendon end-feel
Asymptomatic pain/instability of the shoulder girdle indicating chronic subluxation (sulcus sign)

steps.title: Voluntary Motor Stage classification
steps.description: Classify the patient's stage of hemiplegic recovery and evaluate force output constraints.
expected_findings: Brunnstrom Stage 2 or 3 of motor recovery with mass synergistic execution patterns predominate
Inability to perform isolated joint movements outside the stereotypic flexor or extensor synergies
Weak or absent active hand grip release with complete loss of fine motor dexterity of fingers

steps.title: Upper Motor Neuron Markers
steps.description: Coordinate standardized provocative reflex tests to confirm pyramidal tract lesions.
expected_findings: Positive Babinski sign (extensor plantar response of the great toe with outer toes fanning)
Positive ankle clonus (sustained rhythmical plantarflexion contractions upon sudden passive dorsiflexion)
Positive Hoffman's sign indicating hyper-reflexive cervical spinal segment release

steps.title: Balance, Gait & Disability scoring
steps.description: Apply standard balance indices, assess walking parameters, and score physical ADL independence.
expected_findings: Severe balance disability and high fall risk evidenced by a Berg Balance Scale score < 36/56
Spatiotemporal asymmetry during walking with a circumductive, vaulting, or foot-drop gait pattern
Significant dependency on caregivers for ADLs and self-care (Functional Independence Measure score < 80)

steps.title: Neuro-facilitation & Clinical Staging
steps.description: Analyze clinical inputs to frame a neurologically sound recovery plan.
expected_findings: Diagnostic placement inside Recovery Stages (subacute, hyper-rigid) determining facilitation (Bobath/PNF) bias
Identified long-term motor rehabilitation goals: static/dynamic balance restoration, early ambulation with a quad cane
Defined home exercise program compliance checklist for hemiplegic shoulder preservation, arm support loading, weight transfers

steps.title: Cord Lesion Chronology
steps.description: Trace etiology of injury (fall, motor trauma, infection), diagnostic surgery parameters, and systemic functions.
expected_findings: Complete or incomplete quadriplegia (cervical lesion) or paraplegia (thoracic/lumbar injury)
Inability to sense rectal filling, diminished voluntary rectal contraction/relaxation
Diminished chest expansions, weak cough/clearance mechanism, persistent shortness of breath
Post-operative spinal fusion with orthopedic spinal hardware instrumentation history

steps.title: Bony Integrity & Dermal visual Check
steps.description: Identify muscle mass reduction, pressure sores, clinical line connections, and breathing movements.
expected_findings: Severe muscular atrophy of paralyzed limb segments caudal to the neurological lesion level
Stage I or II pressure injury hazards over vulnerable bony projections (sacrum, heels, scapulae, ischial tuberosities)
Presence of standard clinical attachments: feeding tubes, tracheostomy, urinary Foley catheter bag
Paradoxical breathing patterns: abdominal rise with retraction of intercostal muscles during inhalation

steps.title: Percussion & Autonomic Stability
steps.description: Palpate bony processes, measure thoracic mobility, and log vital autonomic behaviors.
expected_findings: Invasive spinal fracture site tenderness or step-off on gentle spinal palpation
Markedly reduced thoracic chest excursion limits (chest expand < 2 cm at the fourth intercostal gap)
Sudden blood pressure spikes, reflex/bradycardia, profiling autonomic dysreflexia risk (lesions T6 or above)

steps.title: Passive ROM & Tenodesis Bounds
steps.description: Test ranges of lower limb joints and analyze natural hand tenodesis mechanics.
expected_findings: Susceptibility to structural flexion contractures of the hips, knees, and plantarflexion contractures of the ankle
Restricted passive joint movements from progressive muscular spasticity or heterotopic ossification
Functional tenodesis grip preservation: passive finger flexion when wrist extended (for C6/C7 lesions)

steps.title: ASIA Key Muscle MMT Mapping
steps.description: Test manual muscle grades across the 10 bilateral ASIA motor segment key groups.
expected_findings: Complete flaccidity/absence of muscle contraction below the level of the spinal injury (Grade 0)
Specific ASIA motor level determination: the lowest spinal level with a muscle strength ≥ Grade 3
Significant motor scoring disparity marking symmetric paraplegic or tetraplegic loss profiles

steps.title: ASIA Sensory Dermatome Point Testing
steps.description: Test light touch and pin-prick sensations across the 28 bilateral key sensory dermatome areas.
expected_findings: Loss or reduction of superficial sensations (sharp/dull, cotton touch) caudal to the spinal lesion segment
Distinct sensory level identified: the lowest dermatome mapping with perfect scores of 2/2 bilaterally
Complete sensory loss profile mapping or incomplete sparing with variable caudal patch sensation

steps.title: ASIA Scale Grade & Spasticity Metrics
steps.description: Formulate functional scale indices and verify spinal shock status.
expected_findings: Classification of ASIA Impairment Scale (AIS) Grade (Grade A: complete, Grades B-D: incomplete)
Elevated spasticity and reflex muscle spasm after recovery from acute spinal shock
Extremely low physical independence score (Spinal Cord Independence Measure or SCIM score < 40)

steps.title: Strategic Mobility & Autonomic Safeties
steps.description: Plan key strategies for dynamic transfer, bracing, skin security, and autonomic emergency guidelines.
expected_findings: Plan for functional independence (e.g. wheelchair training, transfers) matching the target neural level
Formulated standard precaution checklists: pressure ulcers prevention scheduling, emergency dysreflexia guidelines
Rehab exercises targeting compensatory muscle training, vertical tolerance transitions with a tilt table
